_comment: Template for data/global/slur_responses.json - replies sent when a slur is detected. Each 'responses' entry is a plain string (a 'generic' reply used for every slur) or { content, categories } tagging which slur categories it applies to (names from slurs.json; include 'generic' to also use it everywhere). The pool for a detected slur = all generic replies + every reply tagged its category, then one is picked at random. content can be a GIF/link or text; {user} (author mention) and {count} (their running total) are substituted. 'spam' is sent when a user spams slurs in quick succession. Replies are rate-limited per user. Copy this to slur_responses.json and edit; if absent, the code falls back to a built-in text shame.
responses: 🚨 {user} said a slur. That's slur #{count} for them. Shame.

responses.content: Used for every slur, with extra weight for jewish.
